page: Microsoft Azure B8ms is a burstable virtual machine instance belonging to the BS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports AVX and AVX-512 vector extensions. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 66 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core processing, as measured by stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25% tier, reflecting the burstable nature of the CPU allocation. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth tests, such as Passmark CPU and large-block memory reads, achieve average tier results. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput also align with middle-tier performance profiles, demonstrating stable execution under balanced multi-threaded demands.

The B8ms instance is designed for cost-efficient operations where CPU utilization is typically low to moderate but requires occasional bursts to handle higher loads. This resource-density profile makes it suitable for development environments, low-traffic web servers, small-scale databases, and microservices. It is less suitable for sustained high-compute tasks or large-scale LLM inference due to its burstable CPU allocation and weaker single-core performance metrics.
description: The Microsoft Azure B8ms is an x86_64 burstable virtual machine from the BS family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. It features 8 vCPUs, 8 physical cores operating at 2.6 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 66 GB of local storage with Premium Storage support. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance is optimized for workloads with variable CPU utilization. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. Database operations, Redis SET throughput, and static web serving also perform in the average tier. This hardware configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for environments that do not require continuous full CPU utilization. It is best suited for development environments, build servers, light databases, and low-to-medium traffic web applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B8ms is a burstable virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 66 GB of local storage, offering cost-effective, average multi-core performance for variable workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B8ms is a burstable instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 66 GB local storage, ideal for variable workloads.
tagline: A burstable virtual machine offering balanced memory and multi-core performance for workloads with variable CPU utilization requirements.

32.0 GB of system memory and 66 GB of local storage
Burstable CPU allocation for cost-effective variable workloads
